title: California colleges spend millions on faulty AI systems: 'The chatbot is outdated’
summary: Community colleges are spending millions on AI-powered chatbots that students say often give inaccurate answers. Many might see upgrades this year.
reason: Public institutions are routing student support flows through captured AI systems that systematically destroy the distributed, relational intelligence (counselors, peer networks) they replace — a concrete case of epistemic incompetence of centralization.
angle: Use the 'epistemic incompetence of centralization' tool: colleges aren't just wasting money, they're dismantling the distributed human knowledge infrastructure that actually worked and replacing it with a simulation of support.
